Implement the following plan:

# Enhanced Social Reactions — Implementation Plan

## Context

iskrib_v3 has strong content creation and discovery features but the social interaction layer is thin — reactions are silent, reply threading lacks visual polish, and there's no @mention autocomplete in comments/opinions. These three enhancements make every interaction feel more alive, deepen comment conversations, and lower the friction of mentioning other users — all driving repeat engagement.

---

## Feature 1: Reaction Animations

**Goal:** When a user reacts to a post, show a burst of floating emoji particles that drift upward and fade out from the reaction button.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionButton.jsx` — add `burstState`, capture button rect on react, render burst

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionBurst.jsx` — self-contained portal component

### Design
- 6 emoji particles spawn from `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()`
- Each particle: randomized x drift (-40 to +40px), y travel (-80 to -140px), rotation, scale
- Duration: ~850ms with 60ms stagger per particle
- Rendered via `createPortal(document.body)` to escape card overflow
- Framer Motion `motion.span` for each particle with spring easing
- Auto-cleanup via `setTimeout` → `onComplete` callback → `setBurstState(null)`
- Guard: skip burst on un-react (removing a reaction)
- Guard: `if (burstState) return` to prevent double-burst on rapid clicks
- No CSS file needed — fully inline-styled since it portals out

### Changes to `ReactionButton.jsx`
1. Add `const [burstState, setBurstState] = useState(null)`
2. In `handleQuickClick` (adding reaction path only): capture rect, set burst state
3. In `handlePickerSelect`: capture rect, set burst state
4. Render `{burstState && <ReactionBurst emoji={burstState.emoji} originRect={burstState.rect} onComplete={() => setBurstState(null)} />}`
5. Import `ReactionBurst` and `getReactionEmoji` from `reactionConfig.js` (already imported)

---

## Feature 2: Threaded Reply Improvements

**Goal:** Polish the reply UX with a "Replying to @Name" context chip, animated collapse/expand toggle with count badge, and CSS-class-based indentation.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — add context chip, enhance toggle button, use CSS class for indent
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.css` — add new styles

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/comments/ReplyContextChip.jsx` — small animated chip component

### Changes to `commentsCards.jsx`
1. Import `ReplyContextChip`
2. Inside the `showReplyInput` AnimatePresence block (line 211-242), add `<ReplyContextChip name={comments?.users?.name} onDismiss={(e) => handleClickReplyButton(e)} />` above the textarea
3. Replace the toggle replies button (lines 194-201) with an enhanced version:
   - Animated chevron SVG (`motion.svg` with `rotate: showReplies ? 180 : 0`)
   - Count badge span with `.cm-replies-count-badge` class
   - Text: `{showReplies ? 'Hide replies' : 'View replies'}`
4. Replace `style={{marginLeft: comments?.parent_id ? 20 : 0}}` on line 161 with `className={...cm-card ${comments?.parent_id ? 'cm-card--reply' : ''}}`

### CSS additions to `comments.css`
- `.cm-reply-context-chip` — inline-flex pill with glass morphism, amber `@Name`
- `.cm-reply-context-dismiss` — small X button
- `.cm-replies-count-badge` — amber pill with white count number
- `.cm-card--reply` — `margin-left: 20px`

### Backend: @mention notifications in comments
**File:** `server/services/interactService.js` — `addCommentService`

After successful comment insert, add non-fatal try/catch block:
- Regex `/@([\w-]+)/g` to extract usernames from comment text
- Look up users by username (max 10)
- Filter out `userId` (self) and `receiverId` (post owner, already notified)
- Insert `type: 'mention'` notifications for remaining matched users

---

## Feature 3: @mention Autocomplete

**Goal:** Type `@` in comment/opinion textareas to get a floating user suggestion dropdown with avatar, name, username. Arrow keys + Enter/Tab to select. Inserts `@username ` into the text.

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` — core hook: detects `@query` from text+cursor, debounced search via `searchUsers` API (300ms), manages results/selectedIndex state
- `client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` — textarea glue: onChange/onKeyDown/onKeyUp handlers, dropdown positioning, `handleSelect` (text insertion at cursor), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Tab, Escape)
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` — portaled dropdown with `AnimatePresence`, avatar+name+username rows, mouse hover to highlight
- `client/src/components/mentions/mentionDropdown.css` — glass morphism dropdown matching existing design system

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx` — wire `useTextareaMention` into main comment textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — wire into reply textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx` — wire into opinion textarea, add `MentionDropdown`

### Reused utilities
- `searchUsers()` from `client/API/Api.js` (line 81) — public search, ilike on name+username
- `handleImageFallback` from `client/src/utils/handleImageFallback.js` — avatar fallback
- Existing `MENTION_TRIGGER_REGEX` pattern from Lexical `MentionPlugin.jsx` as reference

### Integration pattern (same for all 3 textareas)
```jsx
const textareaRef = useRef(null);
const { textareaProps, dropdownProps } = useTextareaMention(value, setValue, textareaRef);

<textarea
  ref={textareaRef}
  value={value}
  onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); textareaProps.onChange(e); }}
  onKeyDown={textareaProps.onKeyDown}
  onKeyUp={textareaProps.onKeyUp}
/>
<MentionDropdown {...dropdownProps} />
```

### Edge cases handled
- `maxLength` guard: skip insertion if `newValue.length > maxLength`
- Empty `@` (no query): don't fetch, keep dropdown closed
- CJK composition: skip when `e.isComposing === true`
- Outside click: close dropdown
- Multiple mentions in same text: regex anchored to `$` (end of text-up-to-cursor)

---

## Build Sequence

1. **Reaction Animations** (isolated, zero dependencies)
   - Create `ReactionBurst.jsx`
   - Modify `ReactionButton.jsx`

2. **Reply Threading Polish** (isolated from #1)
   - Create `ReplyContextChip.jsx`
   - Modify `commentsCards.jsx`
   - Add CSS to `comments.css`

3. **Backend mention notifications** (independent)
   - Add @username regex + notification insert to `interactService.js`

4. **@mention Autocomplete** (depends on nothing, but benefits from #3)
   - Create `useMentionAutocomplete.js`, `useTextareaMention.js`, `MentionDropdown.jsx`, `mentionDropdown.css`
   - Wire into `comments.jsx`, `commentsCards.jsx`, `OpinionsEditor.jsx`

---

## Verification

1. **Reactions:** Open feed, react to a post → emoji particles float up from button. Try all 6 reaction types. Un-react (no burst). Long-press on mobile → picker → select → burst. Rapid double-click → only one burst.

2. **Reply threading:** Open comments, click Reply → "Replying to @Name" chip appears above textarea with dismiss X. Click the toggle replies button → animated chevron rotates, count badge visible. Replies indent properly via CSS class.

3. **@mention autocomplete:** Type `@jo` in comment textarea → dropdown appears with matching users. Arrow down/up to navigate, Enter to select → `@johndoe ` inserted. Escape to dismiss. Test in reply textarea and opinion editor. Verify backend: submit comment with `@validusername` → notification created for that user.


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\5e412297-5524-4ae8-82bb-91f789bb77f9.jsonl

# Enhanced Social Reactions — Implementation Plan

## Context

iskrib_v3 has strong content creation and discovery features but the social interaction layer is thin — reactions are silent, reply threading lacks visual polish, and there's no @mention autocomplete in comments/opinions. These three enhancements make every interaction feel more alive, deepen comment conversations, and lower the friction of mentioning other users — all driving repeat engagement.

---

## Feature 1: Reaction Animations

**Goal:** When a user reacts to a post, show a burst of floating emoji particles that drift upward and fade out from the reaction button.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionButton.jsx` — add `burstState`, capture button rect on react, render burst

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionBurst.jsx` — self-contained portal component

### Design
- 6 emoji particles spawn from `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()`
- Each particle: randomized x drift (-40 to +40px), y travel (-80 to -140px), rotation, scale
- Duration: ~850ms with 60ms stagger per particle
- Rendered via `createPortal(document.body)` to escape card overflow
- Framer Motion `motion.span` for each particle with spring easing
- Auto-cleanup via `setTimeout` → `onComplete` callback → `setBurstState(null)`
- Guard: skip burst on un-react (removing a reaction)
- Guard: `if (burstState) return` to prevent double-burst on rapid clicks
- No CSS file needed — fully inline-styled since it portals out

### Changes to `ReactionButton.jsx`
1. Add `const [burstState, setBurstState] = useState(null)`
2. In `handleQuickClick` (adding reaction path only): capture rect, set burst state
3. In `handlePickerSelect`: capture rect, set burst state
4. Render `{burstState && <ReactionBurst emoji={burstState.emoji} originRect={burstState.rect} onComplete={() => setBurstState(null)} />}`
5. Import `ReactionBurst` and `getReactionEmoji` from `reactionConfig.js` (already imported)

---

## Feature 2: Threaded Reply Improvements

**Goal:** Polish the reply UX with a "Replying to @Name" context chip, animated collapse/expand toggle with count badge, and CSS-class-based indentation.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — add context chip, enhance toggle button, use CSS class for indent
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.css` — add new styles

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/comments/ReplyContextChip.jsx` — small animated chip component

### Changes to `commentsCards.jsx`
1. Import `ReplyContextChip`
2. Inside the `showReplyInput` AnimatePresence block (line 211-242), add `<ReplyContextChip name={comments?.users?.name} onDismiss={(e) => handleClickReplyButton(e)} />` above the textarea
3. Replace the toggle replies button (lines 194-201) with an enhanced version:
   - Animated chevron SVG (`motion.svg` with `rotate: showReplies ? 180 : 0`)
   - Count badge span with `.cm-replies-count-badge` class
   - Text: `{showReplies ? 'Hide replies' : 'View replies'}`
4. Replace `style={{marginLeft: comments?.parent_id ? 20 : 0}}` on line 161 with `className={...cm-card ${comments?.parent_id ? 'cm-card--reply' : ''}}`

### CSS additions to `comments.css`
- `.cm-reply-context-chip` — inline-flex pill with glass morphism, amber `@Name`
- `.cm-reply-context-dismiss` — small X button
- `.cm-replies-count-badge` — amber pill with white count number
- `.cm-card--reply` — `margin-left: 20px`

### Backend: @mention notifications in comments
**File:** `server/services/interactService.js` — `addCommentService`

After successful comment insert, add non-fatal try/catch block:
- Regex `/@([\w-]+)/g` to extract usernames from comment text
- Look up users by username (max 10)
- Filter out `userId` (self) and `receiverId` (post owner, already notified)
- Insert `type: 'mention'` notifications for remaining matched users

---

## Feature 3: @mention Autocomplete

**Goal:** Type `@` in comment/opinion textareas to get a floating user suggestion dropdown with avatar, name, username. Arrow keys + Enter/Tab to select. Inserts `@username ` into the text.

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` — core hook: detects `@query` from text+cursor, debounced search via `searchUsers` API (300ms), manages results/selectedIndex state
- `client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` — textarea glue: onChange/onKeyDown/onKeyUp handlers, dropdown positioning, `handleSelect` (text insertion at cursor), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Tab, Escape)
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` — portaled dropdown with `AnimatePresence`, avatar+name+username rows, mouse hover to highlight
- `client/src/components/mentions/mentionDropdown.css` — glass morphism dropdown matching existing design system

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx` — wire `useTextareaMention` into main comment textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — wire into reply textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx` — wire into opinion textarea, add `MentionDropdown`

### Reused utilities
- `searchUsers()` from `client/API/Api.js` (line 81) — public search, ilike on name+username
- `handleImageFallback` from `client/src/utils/handleImageFallback.js` — avatar fallback
- Existing `MENTION_TRIGGER_REGEX` pattern from Lexical `MentionPlugin.jsx` as reference

### Integration pattern (same for all 3 textareas)
```jsx
const textareaRef = useRef(null);
const { textareaProps, dropdownProps } = useTextareaMention(value, setValue, textareaRef);

<textarea
  ref={textareaRef}
  value={value}
  onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); textareaProps.onChange(e); }}
  onKeyDown={textareaProps.onKeyDown}
  onKeyUp={textareaProps.onKeyUp}
/>
<MentionDropdown {...dropdownProps} />
```

### Edge cases handled
- `maxLength` guard: skip insertion if `newValue.length > maxLength`
- Empty `@` (no query): don't fetch, keep dropdown closed
- CJK composition: skip when `e.isComposing === true`
- Outside click: close dropdown
- Multiple mentions in same text: regex anchored to `$` (end of text-up-to-cursor)

---

## Build Sequence

1. **Reaction Animations** (isolated, zero dependencies)
   - Create `ReactionBurst.jsx`
   - Modify `ReactionButton.jsx`

2. **Reply Threading Polish** (isolated from #1)
   - Create `ReplyContextChip.jsx`
   - Modify `commentsCards.jsx`
   - Add CSS to `comments.css`

3. **Backend mention notifications** (independent)
   - Add @username regex + notification insert to `interactService.js`

4. **@mention Autocomplete** (depends on nothing, but benefits from #3)
   - Create `useMentionAutocomplete.js`, `useTextareaMention.js`, `MentionDropdown.jsx`, `mentionDropdown.css`
   - Wire into `comments.jsx`, `commentsCards.jsx`, `OpinionsEditor.jsx`

---

## Verification

1. **Reactions:** Open feed, react to a post → emoji particles float up from button. Try all 6 reaction types. Un-react (no burst). Long-press on mobile → picker → select → burst. Rapid double-click → only one burst.

2. **Reply threading:** Open comments, click Reply → "Replying to @Name" chip appears above textarea with dismiss X. Click the toggle replies button → animated chevron rotates, count badge visible. Replies indent properly via CSS class.

3. **@mention autocomplete:** Type `@jo` in comment textarea → dropdown appears with matching users. Arrow down/up to navigate, Enter to select → `@johndoe ` inserted. Escape to dismiss. Test in reply textarea and opinion editor. Verify backend: submit comment with `@validusername` → notification created for that user.

`addReplyOpinionController` from `uploadController` is imported but never registered as a route — it's dead code. The actual live opinion reply route is `addOpinionReplyController` from `interactController` at line 132. Now I have the complete picture. Here is the full analysis:

---

## Full @mention Notification Analysis

### 1. Reply Flow (`submitReplyController` in `inlineController.js`)

**Route:** `POST /submitReply/:parent_id/:user_id/:post_id/:receiver_id`
**Handler:** `submitReplyController` (lines 74-106 of `/server/controller/inlineController.js`)

This is a completely separate path from `addCommentService`. It does NOT go through `addCommentService` at all. The handler is inline — it directly calls Supabase without any mention-notification logic:

```js
// inlineController.js line 74-106
export const submitReplyController = asyncHandler(async (req, res) => {
    const { parent_id, receiver_id, post_id } = req.params;
    const user_id = req.userId;
    const { reply } = req.body;
    const isOwner = user_id === receiver_id;

    const insertReplyPromise = supabase
        .from('comments')
        .insert({ post_id, user_id, comment: reply, parent_id });

    const insertNotifPromise = supabase
        .from('notifications')
        .insert({ sender_id: user_id, receiver_id, type: 'reply', journal_id: post_id, read: false });

    const [insertNotif, insertReply] = await Promise.all([...]);
    // returns success — NO mention notification logic here
});
```

**Gap confirmed:** `submitReplyController` has zero @mention handling. If a user types `@username` in a reply, no mention notification fires.

---

### 2. Opinion Reply Flow — Two separate services, only one fires notifications

There are **two opinion reply paths**:

**Path A — `addOpinionReplyController` (live, from `interactController.js`)**
- Route: `POST /addOpinionReply/:parent_id/:user_id/:receiver_id`
- Calls `uploadOpinionReplyService` from `interactService.js` (lines 213-258)
- This service DOES send an `opinion_reply` notification to `notification_opinions`
- But has NO mention notification logic either
- Opinions go to `notification_opinions` table (different from `notifications`), so even if you added mention logic it would need special treatment

**Path B — `addReplyOpinionController` (from `uploadController.js`)**

# Enhanced Social Reactions — Implementation Plan

## Context

iskrib_v3 has strong content creation and discovery features but the social interaction layer is thin — reactions are silent, reply threading lacks visual polish, and there's no @mention autocomplete in comments/opinions. These three enhancements make every interaction feel more alive, deepen comment conversations, and lower the friction of mentioning other users — all driving repeat engagement.

---

## Feature 1: Reaction Animations

**Goal:** When a user reacts to a post, show a burst of floating emoji particles that drift upward and fade out from the reaction button.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionButton.jsx` — add `burstState`, capture button rect on react, render burst

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionBurst.jsx` — self-contained portal component

### Design
- 6 emoji particles spawn from `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()`
- Each particle: randomized x drift (-40 to +40px), y travel (-80 to -140px), rotation, scale
- Duration: ~850ms with 60ms stagger per particle
- Rendered via `createPortal(document.body)` to escape card overflow
- Framer Motion `motion.span` for each particle with spring easing
- Auto-cleanup via `setTimeout` → `onComplete` callback → `setBurstState(null)`
- Guard: skip burst on un-react (removing a reaction)
- Guard: `if (burstState) return` to prevent double-burst on rapid clicks
- No CSS file needed — fully inline-styled since it portals out

### Changes to `ReactionButton.jsx`
1. Add `const [burstState, setBurstState] = useState(null)`
2. In `handleQuickClick` (adding reaction path only): capture rect, set burst state
3. In `handlePickerSelect`: capture rect, set burst state
4. Render `{burstState && <ReactionBurst emoji={burstState.emoji} originRect={burstState.rect} onComplete={() => setBurstState(null)} />}`
5. Import `ReactionBurst` and `getReactionEmoji` from `reactionConfig.js` (already imported)

---

## Feature 2: Threaded Reply Improvements

**Goal:** Polish the reply UX with a "Replying to @Name" context chip, animated collapse/expand toggle with count badge, and CSS-class-based indentation.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — add context chip, enhance toggle button, use CSS class for indent
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.css` — add new styles

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/comments/ReplyContextChip.jsx` — small animated chip component

### Changes to `commentsCards.jsx`
1. Import `ReplyContextChip`
2. Inside the `showReplyInput` AnimatePresence block (line 211-242), add `<ReplyContextChip name={comments?.users?.name} onDismiss={(e) => handleClickReplyButton(e)} />` above the textarea
3. Replace the toggle replies button (lines 194-201) with an enhanced version:
   - Animated chevron SVG (`motion.svg` with `rotate: showReplies ? 180 : 0`)
   - Count badge span with `.cm-replies-count-badge` class
   - Text: `{showReplies ? 'Hide replies' : 'View replies'}`
4. Replace `style={{marginLeft: comments?.parent_id ? 20 : 0}}` on line 161 with `className={...cm-card ${comments?.parent_id ? 'cm-card--reply' : ''}}`

### CSS additions to `comments.css`
- `.cm-reply-context-chip` — inline-flex pill with glass morphism, amber `@Name`
- `.cm-reply-context-dismiss` — small X button
- `.cm-replies-count-badge` — amber pill with white count number
- `.cm-card--reply` — `margin-left: 20px`

### Backend: @mention notifications in comments
**File:** `server/services/interactService.js` — `addCommentService`

After successful comment insert, add non-fatal try/catch block:
- Regex `/@([\w-]+)/g` to extract usernames from comment text
- Look up users by username (max 10)
- Filter out `userId` (self) and `receiverId` (post owner, already notified)
- Insert `type: 'mention'` notifications for remaining matched users

---

## Feature 3: @mention Autocomplete

**Goal:** Type `@` in comment/opinion textareas to get a floating user suggestion dropdown with avatar, name, username. Arrow keys + Enter/Tab to select. Inserts `@username ` into the text.

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` — core hook: detects `@query` from text+cursor, debounced search via `searchUsers` API (300ms), manages results/selectedIndex state
- `client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` — textarea glue: onChange/onKeyDown/onKeyUp handlers, dropdown positioning, `handleSelect` (text insertion at cursor), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Tab, Escape)
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` — portaled dropdown with `AnimatePresence`, avatar+name+username rows, mouse hover to highlight
- `client/src/components/mentions/mentionDropdown.css` — glass morphism dropdown matching existing design system

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx` — wire `useTextareaMention` into main comment textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — wire into reply textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx` — wire into opinion textarea, add `MentionDropdown`

### Reused utilities
- `searchUsers()` from `client/API/Api.js` (line 81) — public search, ilike on name+username
- `handleImageFallback` from `client/src/utils/handleImageFallback.js` — avatar fallback
- Existing `MENTION_TRIGGER_REGEX` pattern from Lexical `MentionPlugin.jsx` as reference

### Integration pattern (same for all 3 textareas)
```jsx
const textareaRef = useRef(null);
const { textareaProps, dropdownProps } = useTextareaMention(value, setValue, textareaRef);

<textarea
  ref={textareaRef}
  value={value}
  onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); textareaProps.onChange(e); }}
  onKeyDown={textareaProps.onKeyDown}
  onKeyUp={textareaProps.onKeyUp}
/>
<MentionDropdown {...dropdownProps} />
```

### Edge cases handled
- `maxLength` guard: skip insertion if `newValue.length > maxLength`
- Empty `@` (no query): don't fetch, keep dropdown closed
- CJK composition: skip when `e.isComposing === true`
- Outside click: close dropdown
- Multiple mentions in same text: regex anchored to `$` (end of text-up-to-cursor)

---

## Build Sequence

1. **Reaction Animations** (isolated, zero dependencies)
   - Create `ReactionBurst.jsx`
   - Modify `ReactionButton.jsx`

2. **Reply Threading Polish** (isolated from #1)
   - Create `ReplyContextChip.jsx`
   - Modify `commentsCards.jsx`
   - Add CSS to `comments.css`

3. **Backend mention notifications** (independent)
   - Add @username regex + notification insert to `interactService.js`

4. **@mention Autocomplete** (depends on nothing, but benefits from #3)
   - Create `useMentionAutocomplete.js`, `useTextareaMention.js`, `MentionDropdown.jsx`, `mentionDropdown.css`
   - Wire into `comments.jsx`, `commentsCards.jsx`, `OpinionsEditor.jsx`

---

## Verification

1. **Reactions:** Open feed, react to a post → emoji particles float up from button. Try all 6 reaction types. Un-react (no burst). Long-press on mobile → picker → select → burst. Rapid double-click → only one burst.

2. **Reply threading:** Open comments, click Reply → "Replying to @Name" chip appears above textarea with dismiss X. Click the toggle replies button → animated chevron rotates, count badge visible. Replies indent properly via CSS class.

3. **@mention autocomplete:** Type `@jo` in comment textarea → dropdown appears with matching users. Arrow down/up to navigate, Enter to select → `@johndoe ` inserted. Escape to dismiss. Test in reply textarea and opinion editor. Verify backend: submit comment with `@validusername` → notification created for that user.

# Enhanced Social Reactions — Implementation Plan

## Context

iskrib_v3 has strong content creation and discovery features but the social interaction layer is thin — reactions are silent, reply threading lacks visual polish, and there's no @mention autocomplete in comments/opinions. These three enhancements make every interaction feel more alive, deepen comment conversations, and lower the friction of mentioning other users — all driving repeat engagement.

---

## Feature 1: Reaction Animations

**Goal:** When a user reacts to a post, show a burst of floating emoji particles that drift upward and fade out from the reaction button.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionButton.jsx` — add `burstState`, capture button rect on react, render burst

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/Reactions/ReactionBurst.jsx` — self-contained portal component

### Design
- 6 emoji particles spawn from `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()`
- Each particle: randomized x drift (-40 to +40px), y travel (-80 to -140px), rotation, scale
- Duration: ~850ms with 60ms stagger per particle
- Rendered via `createPortal(document.body)` to escape card overflow
- Framer Motion `motion.span` for each particle with spring easing
- Auto-cleanup via `setTimeout` → `onComplete` callback → `setBurstState(null)`
- Guard: skip burst on un-react (removing a reaction)
- Guard: `if (burstState) return` to prevent double-burst on rapid clicks
- No CSS file needed — fully inline-styled since it portals out

### Changes to `ReactionButton.jsx`
1. Add `const [burstState, setBurstState] = useState(null)`
2. In `handleQuickClick` (adding reaction path only): capture rect, set burst state
3. In `handlePickerSelect`: capture rect, set burst state
4. Render `{burstState && <ReactionBurst emoji={burstState.emoji} originRect={burstState.rect} onComplete={() => setBurstState(null)} />}`
5. Import `ReactionBurst` and `getReactionEmoji` from `reactionConfig.js` (already imported)

---

## Feature 2: Threaded Reply Improvements

**Goal:** Polish the reply UX with a "Replying to @Name" context chip, animated collapse/expand toggle with count badge, and CSS-class-based indentation.

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — add context chip, enhance toggle button, use CSS class for indent
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.css` — add new styles

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/comments/ReplyContextChip.jsx` — small animated chip component

### Changes to `commentsCards.jsx`
1. Import `ReplyContextChip`

3. Replace the toggle replies button (lines 194-201) with an enhanced version:

## Feature 3: @mention Autocomplete

**Goal:** Type `@` in comment/opinion textareas to get a floating user suggestion dropdown with avatar, name, username. Arrow keys + Enter/Tab to select. Inserts `@username ` into the text.

### Files to create
- `client/src/components/mentions/useMentionAutocomplete.js` — core hook: detects `@query` from text+cursor, debounced search via `searchUsers` API (300ms), manages results/selectedIndex state
- `client/src/components/mentions/useTextareaMention.js` — textarea glue: onChange/onKeyDown/onKeyUp handlers, dropdown positioning, `handleSelect` (text insertion at cursor), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Tab, Escape)
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionDropdown.jsx` — portaled dropdown with `AnimatePresence`, avatar+name+username rows, mouse hover to highlight
- `client/src/components/mentions/mentionDropdown.css` — glass morphism dropdown matching existing design system

### Files to modify
- `client/src/components/comments/comments.jsx` — wire `useTextareaMention` into main comment textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx` — wire into reply textarea, add `MentionDropdown`
- `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/OpinionsEditor.jsx` — wire into opinion textarea, add `MentionDropdown`

### Reused utilities
- `searchUsers()` from `client/API/Api.js` (line 81) — public search, ilike on name+username
- `handleImageFallback` from `client/src/utils/handleImageFallback.js` — avatar fallback
- Existing `MENTION_TRIGGER_REGEX` pattern from Lexical `MentionPlugin.jsx` as reference

### Integration pattern (same for all 3 textareas)
```jsx
const textareaRef = useRef(null);
const { textareaProps, dropdownProps } = useTextareaMention(value, setValue, textareaRef);

<textarea
  ref={textareaRef}
  value={value}
  onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); textareaProps.onChange(e); }}
  onKeyDown={textareaProps.onKeyDown}
  onKeyUp={textareaProps.onKeyUp}
/>
<MentionDropdown {...dropdownProps} />
```

### Edge cases handled
- `maxLength` guard: skip insertion if `newValue.length > maxLength`
- Empty `@` (no query): don't fetch, keep dropdown closed
- CJK composition: skip when `e.isComposing === true`
- Outside click: close dropdown
- Multiple mentions in same text: regex anchored to `$` (end of text-up-to-cursor)

4. **@mention Autocomplete** (depends on nothing, but benefits from #3)
   - Create `useMentionAutocomplete.js`, `useTextareaMention.js`, `MentionDropdown.jsx`, `mentionDropdown.css`
